pages.description: Home of Africa’s first generation of female Bitcoin developers Code. Collaborate. Contribute… Explore Pathways Join The Waitlist Home of Africa’s first generation of female Bitcoin developers Code. Collaborate. Contribute… Explore Pathways Join The Waitlist 0 + Alumni Developers 0 + Active Repositories 0 + African Countries 0 + Mentors Our Mission Layer Dada Devs creates pathways for African female engineers to transition into Bitcoin and open-source development. We work with experienced software developers, guiding them to run nodes, build on Lightning, and contribute upstream to Bitcoin repositories. Our focus is code, collaboration, and contribution. Every builder who joins strengthens Bitcoin’s foundation and expands Africa’s role in the open network. Explore Pathways Why Bitcoin? Why Women? Bitcoin is open source code, but its builders don’t yet reflect the world it serves. Africa is driving financial innovation, yet female engineers remain underrepresented in protocol development. Dada Devs exists to close that gap not through theory, but through contribution. We bring overlooked talent into Bitcoin’s core, developers who understand Africa’s systems and can build solutions that keep the network global, inclusive, and unstoppable. Join The Waitlist How We Build Engineering Activation Immersive, code-first onboarding into Bitcoin and Lightning. Ecosystem Collaborations Co-building with global Bitcoin orgs and projects. Open-Source Contribution Real work, commits, pull requests, and repos that move the network forward. Talent Network Connecting skilled engineers to protocol teams and open-source initiatives. Our Partners & Sponsors Become A Partner Alumni Voices Mercy Mbao Dada Devs Alumni After completing the Dada Devs program, I had a pull request merged into the Lightning Network Gallery under BitDevs Nairobi. I also designed a Bitcoin wallet web app prototype using Figma, blending design and technical skills. As a Peer Lead for Cohort 3, I’m currently mentoring fellow developers and fostering collaboration within the community. I’m also expanding my expertise by running functional and unit tests in Bitcoin Core. Annie Adhiambo Dada Devs Alumni At Dada Devs, I worked on YakiSum and co-developed SatsJar, gaining hands-on experience building practical Bitcoin and Web3 solutions. In 2025, Bitcoin Dada helped me turn curiosity into confidence. I joined my first international Web3 hackathon, won the Digital Signature Challenge, and worked on the Bitnob × DadaDev project, showing me how Bitcoin solves real-world problems. The support and sisterhood affirmed that I truly belong in the Dada Dev ecosystem. Grace Mugoiri Dada Devs Alumni I’m Grace Mugoiri, a Dada Devs pioneer. Through Bitcoin Dada, I’m building a Bitcoin game that helps people understand Bitcoin. I’ve gained hands-on experience integrating React Native with Bitcoin, developing full projects from UI/UX to backend systems. My strengths lie in API development, Bitcoin Core integration and creating seamless user experiences. I also run a YouTube channel where I teach developers the technical side of coding in Bitcoin. Busayo Dada Dada Devs Alumni Before Dada Devs, I was curious about Bitcoin development but lacked a technical starting point; the program equipped me with protocol-level fundamentals and hands-on engineering experience across Bitcoin and Lightning projects. I now collaborate on open-source tooling and currently contribute to NodeGaze, an observability platform for Lightning node operators, building multi-node monitoring, real-time event tracking, and webhook-driven insights that strengthen the Bitcoin ecosystem. Wandia Mugo Dada Devs Alumni As a civil-engineer-turned Bitcoin developer, I leveraged the Dada Devs technical program to transition into open-source engineering, building products like PayWithSats and contributing to Lightning, Bitcoin Core, and protocol-level projects. My journey has expanded into software development, protocol research, and technical leadership, earning top-tier Bitcoin certifications, managing developer cohorts, and driving community-focused innovation across the Bitcoin ecosystem. Jane Rose Wambugu Dada Devs Alumni Through the Dada Devs Program, I’ve grown from a Bitcoin learner to a builder. Taking part in a group project, SatSoko, a marketplace inspired by Alibaba but powered by Bitcoin payments, leading the technical direction, helped shape its core features. I’m currently working toward integrating wallet APIs such as Bitnob and BTCPay Server, building solutions that bring Bitcoin closer to everyday commerce and empower global trade through decentralised technology. News and Stories Insights, stories, tutorials, and updates from the Dada Devs ecosystem. See All Breaking Barriers: How Women in Africa are Advancing Bitcoin Development through DADA DEVS February 4, 2025 By Dada Devs The need for more women in Bitcoin development extends far beyond achieving diversity in a male-dominated field; it’s about driving inclusive solutions and ensuring the future of finance reflects the world… Read More → See All Dada Devs February 4, 2025 Breaking Barriers: How Women in Africa are Advancing Bitcoin Development through DADA DEVS The need for more women in Bitcoin development extends far beyond achieving diversity in a male-dominated field; it’s about driving… Read More…

About Us Who We Are… Founded in 2024 by visionary leader Lorraine Marcel, Dada Devs was born from a simple observation. Africa has world-class engineers, yet very few women are contributing to the open-source code that powers Bitcoin. What began as a vision to bridge that gap has evolved into the first initiative dedicated to advancing female engineers into Bitcoin’s open-source ecosystem. We guide experienced developers through the practical side of Bitcoin: understanding the protocol, building on Lightning, running nodes, and collaborating on code that strengthens the network. We build in the open, contribute upstream, and create pathways for engineers to leave a permanent mark on the world’s most resilient financial system. Why We Exist Africa’s financial systems are fragile, yet its talent is extraordinary. Bitcoin offers a foundation for sovereignty and resilience, but the builders behind it don’t yet reflect the people it can serve.Dada Devs exists to close that gap, equipping African female engineers with the technical depth and context to build on Bitcoin itself. We focus on what matters: Understanding the Bitcoin and Lightning stack. Building real applications for African realities. Collaborating across open-source projects. Every builder who joins Dada Devs becomes part of a network proving that African engineers belong at the core of global Bitcoin development. Our Journey So Far Our initial parameters have been stress-tested and the results are chainable. The caliber of talent and the velocity of execution from our earliest builders confirmed a powerful market signal: the demand for deep, protocol-level expertise in Africa is not latent; it’s ready for mainnet. Our development lifecycle: The Forge Where novel ideas are pressure-tested against African reality. This is our fuzzer for financial innovation. The Protocol Deep-Dive Where we replace theoretical knowledge with the muscle memory of C++, Rust, and the Bitcoin Core codebase. This is where builders earn their stripes. The Gossip Protocol A sovereign mesh network of alumni, mentors, and core contributors. This is the persistent layer that ensures knowledge propagates and support is never centralized. Core Pillars of Our Initiative Build Labs & Hackathons Sprint-style projects where ideas become functional tools. Technical Deep Dives Exploring Bitcoin Core, Lightning, and open-source tooling. Community & Continuity An ongoing network of African female Bitcoin developers supporting one another long after each program ends. Mentorship & Collaboration Pairing engineers with maintainers from across the ecosystem. Inside Our Labs https://youtu.be/CHbK-Lottd0 Bitnob X DadaDevs Developers Bootcamp DadaDevs Bootcamp https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jH_1We9Gs1Y Bitcoin Development Bootcamp Panel on Bitcoin and Women in Tech More Proof of Work Meet The Team Dada Devs is powered by passionate open-source contributors, mentors, and developers from across Africa. Lorraine Marcel Founder/C.E.O Wandia Mugo Program Director Precious Okoro Communications & Brand Lead
Application Form Loading…

pages.description: The need for more women in Bitcoin development extends far beyond achieving diversity in a male-dominated field; it’s about driving inclusive solutions and ensuring the future of finance reflects the world it serves. According to the World Bank, about 1.4 billion people globally do not have access to a bank account, and 350 million are...
Contact Us We’d Like To Hear From You… Questions? Inquiries? Kindly get in touch with us. info@dadadevs.com X (Formerly Twitter) Send Us a Message

Builders Directory Meet Our Developers Our alumnae represent the living network of Dada Devs; engineers, designers, and innovators now contributing to Bitcoin, Lightning, and open-source projects across Africa and beyond. Their work bridges innovation and inclusion, shaping the next generation of a decentralized future. Anne Mahonga Software Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Anne Mahonga is a Nairobi-based software developer with experience across web, mobile, and Bitcoin-adjacent systems. She holds a BSc in Information Technology and works with Python, Rust, and React, combining full-stack development with UI/UX decision-making to ship scalable, user-centric products. She is active in open-source and decentralized ecosystems and has a strong track record in applied, competitive build environments. Anne won first place at a Bitnob × Bitcoin Dada hackathon for Bitcab, a ride-hailing application with native Bitcoin payments, and placed third at the Nostr Yakihonne hackathon for Yakisum, an AI-powered long-form article summarizer for Nostr. Her work also includes building ₿Proof for the Bitcoin Dada community — a blockchain-anchored digital certificate system designed to issue, verify, and manage immutable proof of achievement. Anne is currently focused on building practical Bitcoin-native tools that prioritize usability, sovereignty, and real-world adoption. Skills & Stack Python Rust React UI/UX Design

Busayo Dada Backend Developer Lagos, Nigeria X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Busayo Dada is a Backend Developer and Bitcoin Developer based in Nigeria with over 3 years of experience building robust, scalable APIs and web platforms. She holds a Diploma in Software Engineering from AltSchool Africa and has completed Bitcoin Development training through Dada Devs. At Stealth Money, she works on backend infrastructure that powers sovereign Bitcoin savings, developing high-performance APIs that enable users to purchase Bitcoin directly into self-custody and order hardware wallets with reliability and security in mind. She is also an active open-source contributor at NodeGaze, an observability platform for Lightning Network node operators. Her work includes building real-time monitoring dashboards, enabling multi-node management across implementations, and implementing webhook-based notifications for critical alerts and channel liquidity events, supporting node operators with better visibility and control. In addition to her technical work, Busayo serves as a Peer Lead with Dada Devs under the Mastering Bitcoin Pathway, where she mentors and supports developers learning Bitcoin concepts. She leads technical discussions, assists with debugging and code reviews, and breaks down complex ideas into clear, practical explanations, fostering collaboration and growth within the developer community. Her technical expertise includes backend development with Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, and Rust, utilizing frameworks like Spring Boot, NestJS, Node.js, and Express, along with databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB) and deployment tools including Docker and Redis caching. Skills & Stack Java JavaScript TypeScript Rust Spring Boot NextJS Node.js Express MySQL PostgreSQL MongoDB JUnit Mockito Jest Supertest

Esther Iyang Frontend Developer Lagos, Nigeria X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Esther Inyang is a frontend developer based in Lagos, Nigeria, with a B.Sc. in Computer Science and Mathematics (Combined Honours) and over five years of experience across IT and web development. Her technical stack includes HTML, CSS/SCSS, Tailwind, JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Next.js, Redux, Node.js, Express, and MongoDB, with a strong focus on accessibility, UX systems, and scalable frontend architecture. She has completed advanced training including the META React Learning Program, Google Mobile Web Development, and Software Testing & QA by Interswitch. Through the Bitcoin Dada and Dada Devs programs, Esther has been building a solid foundation in Bitcoin and blockchain fundamentals, with a growing focus on how frontend systems interface with decentralized protocols and open-source infrastructure. She is actively working toward contributing to Bitcoin-adjacent and open-source projects, applying strong problem-solving, system thinking, and product intuition to build functional, human-centered interfaces for decentralized technologies. Skills & Stack HTML CSS SCSS Tailwind JavaScript TypeScript React Next.js Redux Node.js Express MongoDB

Jane Rose Wambugu Frontend Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Jane Rose Wambugu is a Full-Stack Developer and QA enthusiast based in Nairobi, Kenya, currently pursuing a Bachelor of Science in Information Technology at KCA University. She has completed specialized certifications including the Dada Devs Bitcoin Development program, Bitcoin Dada program, and FreeCodeCamp courses in C#, Responsive Web Design, and JavaScript Algorithms. Her technical expertise includes full-stack development with C#, JavaScript, and Python, utilizing frameworks like .NET 8/ASP Core, React, Next.js, and Flask, along with databases (Microsoft SQL Server, SQLite) and DevOps tools (Docker, GitHub Actions). Jane Rose demonstrates strong leadership in Agile environments, leading sprint planning and coordinating cross-functional teams while maintaining clear technical communication and project ownership. As an active member of the Dada Devs community, she experiments with Testnet Lightning Network tools and mentors new developers, advocating for financial inclusion through Bitcoin education. Her combination of technical proficiency, user-centered design skills, and collaborative leadership makes her effective at driving projects from conception to delivery while building team cohesion and stakeholder alignment. Skills & Stack C# JavaScript .NET 8/ASP Core React Next.js Flask Microsoft SQL SQLite

Lilian Nwamaife Bitcoin Product Designer Lagos, Nigeria X-twitter Icon-linkedin-1 Icomoon_brands-framer Bio Lilian Nwamaife is a Product Designer and Bitcoin enthusiast based in Lagos, Nigeria, with a strong focus on user-centered design and the transformative potential of Bitcoin technology. She brings a multidisciplinary background, having transitioned from Biochemistry into Product Design, where she leverages analytical thinking and creative problem-solving to craft intuitive digital experiences. She currently works as a Product Designer at Mavapay, a Bitcoin-powered payment infrastructure platform supporting Africa’s entrepreneurial ecosystem. Her design expertise spans across UX research, interaction design, illustration designs, information architecture, prototyping, and responsive web and mobile design (Figma, Adobe XD, Framer, and Miro), with proficiency in HTML and CSS for enhanced design implementation and collaboration with development teams. Lilian has completed specialized training through the Bitcoin Dada Program, the Dev & Design Program ,building expertise across Bitcoin applications, open-source design practices, and decentralized systems. Her work focuses on translating Bitcoin’s technical constraints into clear, secure, and usable product interfaces. She has designed and shipped production products within the ecosystem, including the Dada Devs website and the SatsFlow interface, contributing to user flows, interaction design, and system-level UX decisions aligned with self-custody, security, and trust minimization. Her work sits at the intersection of design and engineering, where usability reinforces protocol realities rather than aesthetics alone. Skills & Stack Figma Framer Miro Adobe XD Prototyping Illustrations UX Research App design App Design Websites HTML CSS

Lucy Wambui Bitcoin Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Lucy Wambui Kamau is a Bitcoin Developer and Data Analyst based in Nairobi, Kenya, and founder of Ishlu Analytics. She holds a Bachelor of Science in Statistics and Computer Science from the Technical University of Mombasa and has completed advanced Bitcoin development programs including Bitcoin Dada Cohort 7, Dada Devs Cohort 1, and the Btrust Builders LBCLI Capstone. Her technical expertise spans Bitcoin Core development (bitcoin-cli, PSBT workflows, multisig transactions), data analysis with Python (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn), SQL, and business intelligence tools. Through Ishlu Analytics, Lucy delivers data-as-a-service solutions to e-commerce businesses while actively contributing to Bitcoin development through open source projects and regtest network tooling. She specializes in making complex data insights accessible to non-technical users and is currently exploring Bitcoin Dev Kit (BDK) for enhanced wallet development. Lucy combines her statistical background with hands-on Bitcoin development skills to advocate for economic empowerment through data and Bitcoin literacy, while mentoring new developers in both fields and seeking opportunities for full-time roles or collaborations in Bitcoin development and data analytics. Skills & Stack Python SQL bitcoin-cli PSBT Workflows NumPy scikit-learn

Mercy Mbao Frontend Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Mercy Mbao is a Frontend Developer and Bitcoin Enthusiast based in Nairobi Kenya with a Bsc in Library and Information science and over 2 years experience in IT support and web development. Her technical skills include HTML, CSS, Javascript, Tailwind, React, Nextjs, Nodejs, Express, python, sql and mongoDB. She has completed Web Development certification by PLP(project power learn), Dada Devs training program cohort 1, B-Trust Builders Pathway 1 mastering bitcoin and language clubs(python). Her growing interest in blockchain integration has led her to contribute to open source projects such as TSK and nexaverse platform, with a keen focus on expanding her skills towards contributing to functional, unit and integration tests in Bitcoin Core. Mercy is passionate about leveraging technology to advance bitcoin education and accessibility. She is a peer lead within Dada Devs, coordinating technical study groups that work through Bitcoin and Lightning implementations, SDK usage, and system design decisions under real-world constraints. Skills & Stack HTML CSS JavaScript Tailwind React Next.js Node.js Express Python MySQL MongoDB

Sharon Nkatha Bitcoin Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Sharon Nkatha is a peer lead at Dada Devs and an emerging Bitcoin developer working at the intersection of open-source technology, education, and financial inclusion. She is currently pursuing a Bachelor’s degree in Software Engineering and is building a Bitcoin e-learning platform for African professionals, focused on bridging traditional careers with the Bitcoinized economy. Sharon has a solid foundation in Bitcoin from the command line, including hands-on experience with Bitcoin Core, transactions, and network concepts. Her technical stack includes JavaScript and the MERN stack, with experience in peer-led development, open-source collaboration, and structured build environments. She holds certifications from Dada Devs, Bitcoin Dada and BitShala and has completed a Business Strategy program at Strathmore University, equipping her to translate Bitcoin technology into practical, scalable solutions for real-world use. Skills & Stack JavaScript Node.js React Express MongoDB

Wandia Mugo Full-stack Developer Nairobi, Kenya X-twitter Icon-github-1 Icon-linkedin-1 Bio Wandia Mugo is a Project Manager at Bitcoin Dada with a diverse technical background spanning Civil Engineering, Full-stack software development, and UX/UI design. Based in Nairobi, Kenya, she holds specialized certifications in Full Stack Development from Moringa School and Bitcoin development through the Dada Devs Bitcoin Development program and Bitshala’s Mastering Bitcoin, Learning Bitcoin from the Command Line and Programming Bitcoin programs. Her technical expertise includes UX/UI,frontend and backend development (Figma,React, JavaScript, Ruby on Rails, Laravel, Python, PHP, Rust), Bitcoin protocol development, Lightning Network applications, Cardano protocol development and user-centered design methodology. Wandia focuses on making Bitcoin technology accessible through education and intuitive application development, particularly for women. As Project Manager for Bitcoin Dada & Dada Devs, she develops Bitcoin education programs, creates user-friendly Bitcoin applications, and mentors emerging developers in the ecosystem. Her work bridges the gap between complex Bitcoin technology and everyday usability, combining engineering precision with inclusive design principles to advance Bitcoin adoption across Africa while building accessible learning materials and open-source contributions to the Bitcoin community. Skills & Stack Ruby on Rails JavaScript Laravel Python Figma PHP Rust React

pages.pageTitle: Dada Devs 2025 Impact Review: Technical Capacity in Practice
pages.description: If you spend enough time inside a developer program, years do not announce themselves. They end quietly, usually in the middle of another build, another review, another session that runs longer than planned. That was 2025 for Dada Devs. Most of the year was spent doing the work. Running cohorts. Supporting builds. Working through unclear...

pages.pageTitle: Introducing Dada Hub: Africa’s First Physical Bitcoin Developer Space
pages.description: Bitcoin adoption in Africa has largely been driven by necessity: unstable currencies, surveillance, restricted access to global markets...

Join The Team DADA DEVS is growing. We’re looking for thinkers, designers, and engineers who understand Bitcoin, care about open-source, and want to help us tell this story from Africa to the world. Current Openings Work with the builders behind Bitcoin’s next chapter. Communications & Branding Lead Contract based | Remote, Nairobi (Preferred) View Details

Communications & Branding Lead Location: Remote / Hybrid (Nairobi preferred) Type: Contract based About DADA DEVS DADA DEVS is building Africa’s first pipeline of female open-source Bitcoin engineers. We train, mentor, and connect African female engineers to contribute directly to the Bitcoin and Lightning Network, shaping the future of financial freedom through open-source work. We’re not a company. We’re a network of builders redefining what innovation looks like from the Global South. About the Role We’re looking for a Communications & Branding Lead to define how DADA DEVS shows up to the world, across voice, visuals, and storytelling. You’ll build the bridge between deep technical work and public understanding, turning our ecosystem’s mission into a clear, consistent, and magnetic narrative. You’ll lead brand communication, manage our content ecosystem, and drive engagement across our digital platforms, from strategy to execution. What You’ll Do Develop and maintain DADA DEVS’ brand voice and visual identity across all media. Design and execute communication strategies for launches, events, and partnerships. Manage social media channels (Twitter, LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube), including content creation, posting, analytics, and community engagement. Oversee design direction for visual content (graphics, motion, videos) in collaboration with the creative team. Draft and edit copy for press releases, newsletters, blogs, and campaigns. Coordinate cross-platform storytelling to highlight our builders, programs, and impact. Collaborate closely with leadership to ensure brand alignment across all initiatives. What You Bring 2+ years of experience in communications, branding, or marketing strategy (preferably in tech, finance, or open-source ecosystems). Proven ability to manage digital platforms and grow authentic engagement. Strong writing and storytelling skills, you can make complex ideas feel human. Experience with tools like Canva, Figma, Notion, or scheduling and analytics platforms. Understanding of Bitcoin or the broader open-source culture (a plus, not a dealbreaker). High attention to detail, self-directed, and comfortable working in fast-paced, remote-first teams. You’ll Fit Right In If You: See communication as code, every post, every word, every colour is a commit. Believe Bitcoin is more than tech, it’s a social shift. Thrive in minimal structure and maximum purpose. Can move between strategy and execution without ego. Why Join Us Be part of one of Africa’s fastest-growing Bitcoin initiatives. Work with an international network of open-source builders and educators. Build your personal brand within a global movement redefining financial systems. Contribute to real impact, every narrative you shape amplifies inclusion and freedom. How to Apply Submit your application through our Careers Form. Include links to your portfolio, previous work, or any brand projects you’ve led. DADA DEVS is an equal opportunity builder network. We value skill, curiosity, and proof-of-work over titles. Apply Now

Communications & Branding Lead DADA DEVS is building Africa’s first pipeline of female open-source Bitcoin engineers, and we’re expanding our core team. The Communications & Branding Lead will shape how our story is told across continents, defining visual tone, managing brand alignment, and driving communication strategy for our ecosystem. This role is for someone who can translate complex ideas into clear narratives, understands Bitcoin culture, and can bridge technical insight with creative expression. Complete the form below to apply.

Mentor With Us Teach from experience. Build through others. Code Reviews Lead small groups through pull requests, debugging, and testing Bitcoin or Lightning projects. Workshops Run hands-on sessions or Lightning labs to deepen developer understanding. Project Guidance Help builders scope, design, and ship open-source tools. 1 on 1 Mentorship Offer weekly or monthly check-ins with selected devs. Apply to Mentor A few quick fields. High signal. How Mentorship Works Connect We match mentors with devs by focus area. Collaborate Host sessions, reviews, or labs. Contribute Track progress and ship open-source work. We only store what you submit. No marketing emails. Frequently Asked Questions Who can mentor? Active Bitcoin or Lightning developers, designers, or open-source contributors. How long is the commitment? Flexible, from one session to ongoing support. Is this paid? Most mentorships are voluntary; sponsored collaborations are arranged case by case.

Partner With Us Host workshops, share tools, or co-develop open-source with Dada Devs. Workshops & Labs Run protocol-level sessions or deep dives for Bitcoin & Lightning devs. Tools & Infrastructure Provide SDKs, APIs, nodes, or test environments for learning. Co-dev / Open Source Collaborate on issues, PRs, and experimental repos. Hiring & Placements Offer internships or contributor roles for our devs. Start a Partnership Minimal fields. High signal. How Our Partnership Runs Scope Define session, resources, or repo. Build Run the lab or co-dev sprint. Ship Merge PRs, publish results, debrief. We only store what you submit. No marketing emails. Frequently Asked Questions What counts as a workshop? 60–120 min, code-first, demo or lab format. Can we bring our SDK or tooling? Yes. Include docs or sandbox access. Do you do NDAs? Only when absolutely necessary for unreleased work.

Choose your Track. Build your Layer. Find Your Muse Our Tracks are designed for engineers ready to go beyond the surface and work directly within Bitcoin’s open-source stack. Each track is a focused technical pathway from understanding Bitcoin at the protocol layer to building on Lightning, managing open repositories, or shaping user experience in decentralized systems.Find your Muse. Mastering Bitcoin Understand the code that started it all. Dive into Bitcoin’s architecture from the inside out, transactions, blocks, nodes, and consensus. You’ll learn how Bitcoin actually works under the hood and why its design is unstoppable. By the end, you’ll not only use Bitcoin, you’ll think in Bitcoin. Focus areas: Bitcoin protocol, transactions, UTXOs, nodes, consensus, privacy, and security. My Muse Mastering Lightning Because freedom should move at the speed of light. Learn how the Lightning Network makes Bitcoin scalable, instant, and global. Build payment channels, understand routing, and experiment with real nodes. This is for builders who believe Bitcoin isn’t just money but also communication. Focus areas: Payment channels, HTLCs, routing, node management, Lightning app development. My Muse Programming Bitcoin Code that speaks the Bitcoin language. Start from first principles, build Bitcoin from scratch using Python, just as Jimmy Song teaches. Understand every function, hash, and signature until you can recreate the system yourself. It’s not about memorising code, it’s about mastering logic. Focus areas: Cryptography, transactions, digital signatures, serialization, Python scripting for Bitcoin. My Muse UI/UX for Bitcoin Design that respects sovereignty. Bitcoin interfaces should be simple, secure, and human. In this pathway, you’ll design with intention, crafting experiences that make self-custody intuitive and accessible. You’ll merge aesthetics with protocol-level understanding. Focus areas: User experience design, wallet flows, usability for emerging markets, accessibility, and trustless design principles. My Muse Git & GitHub Your gateway to open source. Every builder contributes to something bigger. Learn how to use Git the right way, from commits and branches to pull requests and collaboration. This is how your work lives on-chain, not the blockchain, but the chain of builders before you. Focus areas: Git fundamentals, branching, collaboration workflows, open-source contribution, and code review culture. My Muse Solo Node For self-paced builders verifying their own path. This is where independent builders begin. A curated reading path for those who learn by doing, and verify their own understanding. From whitepapers to code walkthroughs, each resource in this list is open-source, timeless, and designed to deepen your connection to Bitcoin’s architecture. My Muse Meet Our Tutors The minds guiding our tracks are active contributors, engineers, and builders shaping Bitcoin across Africa and beyond. Each tutor brings real-world experience, from running nodes and building Lightning apps to contributing code and designing user experiences that make Bitcoin more accessible. Together, they form the human layer of our open-source mission. Munirat Software Engineer Lamar Software Engineer Erik Bitcoin Product Designer Peter Open Source Contributor Mogashi Bitcoin UX Researcher Theophilus Open Source Contributor Jennifer Software Engineer Tobechi Software Engineer Tobi Software Engineer Vayras Bitcoin Developer and Bitshala Fellow Frequently Asked Questions What is Dada Devs? Dada Devs is a network for African female engineers building on Bitcoin and Lightning. We guide experienced developers into open-source contribution, running nodes, shipping Lightning apps, and working on real Bitcoin repos. Do I need to know how to code before joining? Yes. Dada Devs is designed for developers who already code in at least one language (Python, JavaScript, C++, Rust, etc.).We don’t teach programming fundamental, we teach Bitcoin development. What will I learn? You’ll learn how Bitcoin works at the protocol level, how to build on Lightning, use GitHub effectively, and contribute to open-source projects.Each cohort focuses on practical builds, not theory. Who can apply? Engineers and developers across Africa with experience in software development who want to specialize in Bitcoin.We prioritize female developers, but male allies can participate in select labs and hackathons. Is there a cost to join? No. Dada Devs programs are fully sponsored through community and partner support. You only need your time, commitment, and a laptop that can run a full Bitcoin node. How are participants selected? Applications are reviewed based on technical background, motivation, and alignment with open- source principles.We value curiosity and contribution over titles. What kind of time commitment is required? Each cohort runs for 4-8 weeks with weekly sessions, labs, and peer projects. Expect to dedicate 4-6 hours per week. What happens after I complete the program? Graduates join our builder network, a community of Bitcoin developers, maintainers, and contributors working on open-source projects across Africa.You’ll have access to mentorship, opportunities, and collaborations with global Bitcoin teams. Can I volunteer or mentor instead of joining as a participant? Yes. We welcome experienced Bitcoin developers, designers, and educators as mentors. You can host technical sessions, code reviews, or join our open-source sprints. How do I apply? Visit dadadevs.com and click “Apply to Build.”You’ll fill out a short form about your background, experience, and technical focus (Bitcoin, Lightning, UI/UX, GitHub).

Git & Open Source Collaboration Duration: 6 Weeks The backbone of contribution. Learn the habits, workflows, and etiquette of open-source engineering. Focus Areas Git basics and branching strategies Pull requests, issues, and reviews Repository management Building credibility through contribution What You’ll Build A contribution trail, your first merged PRs and documented commits. Outcome Integration into the open-source network that builds Bitcoin. Join the Waitlist

Mastering Bitcoin Overview The Mastering Bitcoin Track is the foundational course of Dada Devs, designed to equip African female engineers with the technical knowledge and skills to engage directly with Bitcoin’s open-source ecosystem. Anchored on Mastering Bitcoin (3rd Edition) by Andreas M. Antonopoulos, the program is structured as a progressive deep dive into the Bitcoin protocol, from keys and transactions to consensus mechanisms, scripting, and second-layer scaling solutions. Participants are expected not only to understand theory but also to implement concepts, run nodes, interact with Bitcoin Core, and collaborate on GitHub. Duration: 8 Weeks The base layer. Understand Bitcoin at its core, consensus, scripting, and transaction flow. Learn how blocks are built, verified, and broadcast. This track is for engineers who want to speak the language of the protocol, not just use it. Focus Areas Network architecture and node operation. Keys, scripts, and transactions. Consensus rules and validation logic. Privacy, security, and scalability layers What You’ll Build Run a full node. Trace transactions from mempool to block. Simulate consensus. End with practical fluency in how Bitcoin actually works. Outcome Protocol literacy. The foundation for every other track. Curriculum Schedule Week 1 – Introduction & Foundations Intro to Cohort: Welcome & orientation Program overview (8 weeks structure) Importance of open source in Bitcoin Tools setup (Bitcoin Core, GitHub, etc.) A developer trainer is needed for the tools setup, others can be handled through peer learning What is Bitcoin: Resource: Intro to Bitcoin & How Bitcoin Works Bitcoin basics, history, Satoshi’s vision Properties of Bitcoin (decentralized, censorship-resistant, borderless) Real-world use cases in Africa A developer is not required for this section. Can be handled through peer learning. Week 2 – Bitcoin Core, Keys, Wallets & Transactions Resource: Bitcoin Core, Keys & Addresses, Wallets & Transactions Bitcoin Core: Bitcoin Core Development Environment Configuring the Core Build Running a Bitcoin Node A developer is needed for this session Keys, Addresses, Wallets: Private/public keys, addresses Wallet types (hot, cold, custodial, non-custodial) HD wallets, mnemonics (BIP32, BIP39, BIP44) A developer is needed for this session Transactions: UTXO model explained Transaction structure: inputs, outputs, digital signatures SegWit overview A developer is needed for this session Week 3 – Advanced Bitcoin Resources: Authentication & Authorization, Signatures & Fees Scripting & Smart Contracts: Bitcoin Script basics Common scripts (P2PKH, P2SH, P2WPKH) Multisig, timelocks, HTLCs Privacy & Security: Wallet security & cold storage Threat models (malware, phishing, physical attacks) Privacy techniques (CoinJoin, address reuse) A developer is a must for this session Week 4 – Network & Blockchain Resource: Network & Blockchain Bitcoin Network: P2P architecture, nodes Network discovery, mempool, propagation Full nodes vs SPV/light clients A developer will be really great but not mandatory Blockchain Basics: Block structure (headers, Merkle trees) Validation rules, longest chain rule Forks (soft vs hard) A developer is not mandatory Week 5 – Mining & Consensus Resource: Mining & Security Proof of Work & Mining: Mining process & hardware evolution Difficulty adjustment & incentives (block rewards, fees) A developer will be great but not required Consensus & Security: 51% attacks, chain reorgs Economic security of Bitcoin Governance & upgrades A developer is not mandatory Week 6 – Scaling & Applications Resource: Second Layer Applications Lightning Network: Payment channels How Lightning scales Bitcoin Real-world examples in Africa Bitcoin Ecosystem: Sidechains, testnets, regtest Bitcoin-based applications & integrations Bitcoin culture, politics, financial inclusion A developer is needed for this session Weeks 7–8 – Projects & Peer Learning Project Kickoff: Students form groups/individual projects Define scope: wallet demos, scripts, research papers, Lightning apps Peer Learning Session: Students lead discussions on topics of interest (Git/GitHub, mining in Africa, Bitcoin regulation) Project Development Support: Mentorship check-in Debugging, GitHub submissions Final Project Presentations: Showcase session Peer + mentor feedback Closing reflections & next steps Teaching Strategy Core Lectures (Mondays/Wednesdays): Delivered by trainers. Peer Learning: Git/GitHub basics, Bitcoin politics, and culture → assigned to study groups. Projects: Encourage hands-on, open-source contributions (small PRs, Lightning experiments, research reports). Join The Waitlist

Mastering Lightning Duration: 8 Weeks Bitcoin’s second layer: built for speed, scale, and sovereignty. Understand how payment channels work, how routing happens, and how Lightning brings Bitcoin to everyday use. Focus Areas Channel creation, closure, and routing HTLCs and onion routing Building Lightning-enabled applications Node management and network integration What You’ll Build Deploy a Lightning node. Open and close channels. Build a simple Lightning app that moves real sats. Outcome Live experience in the layer where Bitcoin meets real-time. Join the Waitlist

Programming Bitcoin Duration: 8 Weeks Bitcoin through code. Rebuild core logic piece by piece to understand the system from inside out. This track turns curiosity into precision. Focus Areas Python, C++, and Rust implementations Transaction serialization and signing Hash functions and elliptic curve cryptography Recreating Bitcoin primitives from scratch What You’ll Build A minimal Bitcoin node prototype. Your own tools to sign, broadcast, and verify transactions. Outcome Technical fluency and confidence to contribute upstream. Join the Waitlist

Solo Node (In Development) Duration: Flexible · Self-Directed For self-paced builders verifying their own path. What You’ll Explore Bitcoin Whitepaper · Mastering Bitcoin · Programming Bitcoin Lightning Network Docs · Bitcoin Design Guide Selected repos, podcasts & talks Tools Outcome Build understanding on your own terms. Knowledge is open-source. Join the Waitlist

UI/UX for Bitcoin Systems Duration: 6 Weeks Design meets protocol. This track explores how people experience Bitcoin, and how thoughtful interfaces make complex systems usable, safe, and trusted. Focus Areas UX for wallets and payment apps Visual systems for open-source tools Accessibility and localization in African contexts Security through design clarity What You’ll Build Prototypes for Bitcoin and Lightning products that prioritize function and user trust. Outcome Designs that simplify self-custody and reflect the human side of Bitcoin. Apply Now
